key_departures.impact: 10-year franchise LT with 145 career starts requested release after contract talks broke down. Lions wanted him to take a pay cut from his $18.2M 2026 salary. Leaves a gaping hole at LT opposite Penei Sewell. Only Gio Manu and Devin Cochran remain as depth options, neither capable of starting. $9.4M dead cap absorbed; $11.6M cap savings.
Released in cap-saving move. Glasgow was pressed into a full-time starting role out of position last season when Ragnow retired. Departure further depletes an already-thin interior OL.

Multi-year defensive captain signed 2yr/$17M with Bucs. Leaves a starter-level hole beside Jack Campbell. Rodriguez re-signed as depth but is not a like-for-like starter replacement. Campbell (All-Pro, 178 tackles) anchors the room but Anzalone's leadership and run-stopping role must be replaced.

Signed 2yr/$16M with Commanders after two seasons in Detroit. Was Detroit's most reliable CB depth piece and took on a near-starter role given injury losses in the secondary. Departure deepens CB concerns, especially with Brian Branch (Achilles) and Kerby Joseph (knee) coming off season-ending injuries.
Signed 2yr/$11.5M to return to Arizona. Had 2 sacks in his lone Detroit season. Rotational IDL loss that was one of last offseason's best bargain signings. Thins a DT room that was already a need.
Signed 2yr/$6.1M with Bills. Backup QB vacancy opened. Bridgewater subsequently re-signed, so this hole was quickly addressed.
Signed 1yr/$5.1M with Bears after five seasons in Detroit. Primarily a depth/ST contributor. Marginal need impact — Lions have Amon-Ra St. Brown and Jameson Williams at the top.

Traded to Houston for Juice Scruggs + 2026 4th + 2027 7th. Montgomery had 716 yards on 158 carries in 2025; team had been leaning heavily on Gibbs anyway. Created RB2 vacancy immediately filled by Pacheco signing.
Veteran swing tackle depth now gone. Further depletes tackle room already missing Decker.

signings.analysis: Three-year deal with $14M guaranteed gives Mays two effective years of real money — a real investment but not quite a franchise-level commitment. At $8.3M AAV he is the 8th-highest paid center in the NFL — solid starter-tier value. He was Detroit's top target and immediately becomes the projected Week 1 starter, solving the vacancy left by Ragnow's retirement and Glasgow's release. His 98.6 PFF pass-blocking efficiency and just one sack allowed in 756 pass-blocking snaps over the last two seasons is encouraging. However, he has limited full-time starting experience (27 career starts) and Juice Scruggs also provides interior depth competition. IOL drops from Tier 1 critical to Tier 2/3 — center is addressed; guard depth still thin enough to warrant a Day 3 draft pick or additional signing.

Acquired via trade (David Montgomery to Houston for Scruggs + 2026 4th-round pick + 2027 7th-round pick). Scruggs is on the final year of his rookie deal with $0 guaranteed remaining — a purely cost-free depth add. He has 1,663 career snaps at IOL and can play guard and center, but ranked in the bottom five of PFF grades among guards in 2025. Provides swing/depth insurance behind Mays and Tate Ratledge but does not resolve the guard depth need. The draft picks received (especially the 4th) are the real value here.

One-year, $2.5M deal with no reported guarantees. Borom has 38 career starts including 11 at right tackle for Miami in 2025, with a 91.2% pass block win rate (32nd among qualified tackles). His experience gives him a theoretical path to starting but he is realistically a functional depth piece and swing option, not a true LT solution opposite Sewell. OT need is essentially unchanged — the Lions still must address the starting LT vacancy in the draft. This signing is Dan Skipper's replacement in terms of depth role, not Decker's replacement.

One-year prove-it deal with exact financials not yet disclosed. Pacheco, 27, slots in as the RB2 behind Jahmyr Gibbs, replacing the role vacated by trading David Montgomery. He is a physical, tough runner (2,537 career rushing yards, 14 TDs with the Chiefs) who profiles as an ideal complement to the elusive Gibbs. However, he's coming off a broken fibula in 2024 and a down 2025 (3.9 YPC, 462 yards in 13 games), and the one-year structure signals a bridge/prove-it arrangement. The Lions may revisit RB depth in the draft. RB need drops from Tier 1 (vacancy after Montgomery trade) to Tier 3/4 — depth need remains but the starter role is filled.

One-year, $4M re-signing. Ya-Sin can play outside and in the slot and even offers safety flexibility — critical given Brian Branch (Achilles) and Kerby Joseph (knee) are recovering from season-ending injuries. He partially offsets the Amik Robertson departure with familiarity in the system. However, the one-year structure and below-starter AAV signals this is a bridge/depth solution. The Lions still have massive uncertainty in the secondary and CB/S remain significant draft needs.

One-year deal; financial terms not reported, likely near-minimum. Rodriguez was a fan favorite returning from a torn ACL — he played only seven games in 2025 with 12 tackles and one TFL. He is a depth piece who can contribute on special teams and rotational snaps, not an Anzalone starter-level replacement. With Jack Campbell (All-Pro) anchoring the room and Derrick Barnes as the other starter, the LB corps needs a true starter-caliber Anzalone replacement via FA or draft. LB remains a meaningful need.

One-year deal; financial terms not reported. Bridgewater previously backed up Goff in 2023 and 2024 and is a trusted locker-room presence and mentor. Fully addresses the backup QB need created by Kyle Allen's departure to Buffalo. He is not a starter-tier player but the Lions have Jared Goff as the unquestioned starter and simply needed a capable, system-familiar backup. Backup QB need resolved.

Lions tendered Bates as an exclusive rights free agent ahead of the start of the new league year. Kicker was not a pre-FA need. Retention is routine — Bates showed promise in 2025 despite slight efficiency dip.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Taylor Decker's release (10-year LT, 145 starts) leaves Detroit with no viable starting LT opposite Penei Sewell. Larry Borom is a functional depth signing on a one-year deal — explicitly a stopgap. Gio Manu and Devin Cochran are developmental backups. This is now the most critical position on the roster for the draft. OT must be targeted in rounds 1-2.

Cade Mays (3yr/$25M, $14M gtd) is a legitimate starter investment and immediate solution at center. Juice Scruggs adds depth insurance on a $0-guaranteed deal. Graham Glasgow's release is absorbed. Tate Ratledge stays at RG. IOL center spot is addressed; guard depth is thin but manageable. Still worth a Day 3 flier.

Isiah Pacheco (1yr, terms TBD) fills the RB2 vacancy left by the David Montgomery trade and is a quality complementary back to Gibbs. However, the one-year prove-it structure means this is not a long-term solution. Sione Vaki stays as RB3. Lions could still take a late-round RB for development but it is no longer a priority.

Rock Ya-Sin's one-year re-signing partially offsets Amik Robertson's departure but does not fully resolve the need. Brian Branch (Achilles) and Kerby Joseph (knee) are uncertain for the start of 2026. Terrion Arnold is the projected CB1 but depth and nickel questions remain serious. CB remains a strong draft priority in rounds 2-4.

Alex Anzalone's departure (2yr/$17M to Bucs) removes the starting WLB and a multi-year defensive captain. Malcolm Rodriguez's one-year re-signing is depth-only. Jack Campbell (All-Pro) and Derrick Barnes anchor the room but need a true third/starter-caliber LB alongside them. Lions should address this in rounds 3-5 or via a second-wave FA signing.

Zero movement on EDGE in free agency. Al-Quadin Muhammad remains unsigned (unsigned/free agent as of now). Aidan Hutchinson is the locked-in starter (4yr/$180M) but the Lions desperately need a No. 2 EDGE opposite him. This was the biggest pre-FA need and remains completely unaddressed. Must be a top-3 draft target or secured via second-wave FA.

Roy Lopez's departure (2yr/$11.5M to Arizona) removes a rotational DT who had 2 sacks in his lone Detroit season. Alim McNeill remains the starter but depth behind him is thin after losing Lopez. No replacement signed. IDL depth is a genuine need that should be addressed in rounds 3-5 or via minimum deals.

Brian Branch (Achilles) and Kerby Joseph (knee) are both coming off season-ending injuries with uncertain 2026 availability. Rock Ya-Sin's versatility provides some insurance but the Lions remain extremely thin at safety depth. No dedicated S signing made. This position warrants draft attention in rounds 3-5.

Teddy Bridgewater re-signed to back up Goff, replacing Kyle Allen (Bills). Bridgewater knows the system, is a locker-room leader, and has backed up Goff before. Backup QB need resolved. Not a draft priority.

key_additions.headline: Top center target fills the Frank Ragnow void with $14M guaranteed and starter-quality pass protection stats
Interior OL depth added as part of David Montgomery trade haul alongside a 2026 4th-round pick
Two-time Super Bowl champion steps in as David Montgomery's replacement alongside Jahmyr Gibbs
Veteran swing tackle with 38 career starts provides OL depth after Taylor Decker's shocking release
Versatile DB re-signed to stabilize injury-decimated secondary amid Branch and Joseph recovery uncertainty
Fan-favorite 'Rodrigo' returns on one-year deal to provide LB depth after Anzalone's departure
Familiar backup and locker-room mentor returns to fill Kyle Allen's vacancy behind Jared Goff

key_losses.headline: Shock departure of 10-year franchise LT (145 starts) leaves gaping hole at left tackle opposite Penei Sewell
Popular RB2 traded in cap-saving move, breaking up the Gibbs-Montgomery backfield tandem
Multi-year defensive captain and LB starter departs, leaving a leadership and production void beside Jack Campbell
Most reliable CB depth piece departs, deepening secondary concerns with Branch and Joseph both rehabbing
Veteran interior lineman cut in cap move, completing the Lions' dramatic pre-FA offensive line teardown
One of last year's best bargain acquisitions returns to Cardinals, leaving Lions DT rotation thinner
Backup QB departs after impressing in preseason; quickly replaced by Teddy Bridgewater reunion
Five-year Detroit favorite joins former OC Ben Johnson's Bears in one-year deal

notes: Pacheco and Bridgewater contract financials not yet fully disclosed as of 2026-03-11; AAVs estimated as near-veteran-minimum/prove-it tier based on reports of one-year structure. Mays contract figures confirmed by Spotrac, OverTheCap, and multiple credible reporters (Aaron Wilson). Borom confirmed at 1yr/$2.5M per SI tracker. All departures confirmed by multiple credible beat reporters and NFL Network insiders.
